title: Here’s the ‘ascending prospect’ the Patriots are projected to take in the NFL Draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Morning Sports Update "Iheanachor is a mauler with the size, strength and athleticism to flourish as a heavy-handed right tackle.
" Max Iheanachor runs the 40-yard dash at the NFL rookie combine.
He is projected as a possible Patriots pick in recent mock drafts.

title: Mass. teens rescued from near the top of Mount Washington
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News One of the teens reported experiencing leg pain, as crews raced worsening weather to reach them.
The summit of New Hampshire's Mount Washington.
Robert F.

title: Man killed, officer shot during traffic stop in New Hampshire
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The deadly officer-involved shooting occurred Monday evening in Ashland, New Hampshire.
A police officer was wounded and a man was shot and killed during a traffic stop in Ashland, New Hampshire, on Monday, according to Attorney General John Formella.
Members of the Ashland Police Department pulled over a car on Main Street Monday evening.

title: Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte reportedly mentioned in trade talks this offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots "With Romeo Doubs coming in and the eventual AJ Brown trade, teams believe he could be available for the right price.
" Kayshon Boutte was Drake Maye's go-to deep threat during the 2025 season.
AP Photo/Greg M.

title: Martin Richard’s brother finishes his 3rd Boston Marathon
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Marathon "He's in my mind every day, especially on a meaningful day like today," Henry Richard said at the Boston Marathon finish line on Monday.
Henry Richard crosses the finish line of the Boston Marathon on the ten year anniversary of the Marathon bombing in 2023.
(Jessica Rinaldi/The Boston Globe) ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: At Rochambeau, Boston Marathon runners met with applause and cowbells
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston Marathon Cheering on runners isn't just for the course.
At restaurants around Boston, runners get ovations when they walk in.
Rochambeau was bustling on Marathon Monday, with runners and spectators celebrating the world's oldest road race.

title: James Hagens and Bruins’ ‘kid line’ received a seal of approval after playoff debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "They were our best defensive line yesterday, I thought.
" James Hagens and the Bruins' rookie third line held their own in Buffalo on Sunday.

title: After the Boston Marathon, the clam chowder ritual begins
